last_item: Mode-S discovery/convergence pass (backlog actionable-empty after iter 22). 5-agent parallel panel, same coverage as iters 15-22 (Dashboard+Query; Roles+Backups+BackupStorageForm; Alerts+Migrate; Settings+Tuning+Pooler+Login; nav/IA+first-run+consistency), deliberately HIGH bar, given the full shipped/rejected digest.
last_result: CONVERGED — no new high/med item; second stable_streak increment 1 -> 2/3. All five views converged. Three candidates surfaced and ALL collapsed on code-level inspection (self-rejected, no panel — iter-5/13/14 precedent): (1) Query 'clear results panel when SQL changes' — FALSE PREMISE about expected behavior: the proposed useEffect(...,[sql]) fires on every keystroke and would wipe the result table the moment you refine a query (add WHERE/fix typo), hostile to run->read->refine->run; the persisted result is the universal SQL-console convention (psql/pgAdmin/DataGrip/Jupyter/devtools all keep last-run output while composing the next query); the panel means 'last run', not 'current editor text', and carries its own row-count/duration. Agent itself ended on CONVERGED. (2) Backups '"Back up now" destination awareness' — agent self-rated LOW payoff (destination badge already in header, run is confirmed); not promoted. (3) Backups 'redundant empty-state messaging' — intentional two-layer design on inspection (danger Callout = status warning; EmptyState = next step), not duplication; agent ended on CONVERGED. Docs-only iteration (backlog/learnings/progress/state), no code shipped, no hard gates needed. LESSON: before 'fixing' stale UI state, ask whether the staleness is the EXPECTED CONVENTION for that control — a read-only results/output panel that persists across edits is a feature (the edit-and-rerun loop depends on it), not a bug; clearing-on-change is right only when the stale value could be acted on as if current. Backlog still actionable-empty (NEEDS-BACKEND badge item + low/watch nits). Next iteration: run the FINAL Mode-S convergence check; one more clean pass -> write COMPLETE.md and stop the loop.
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn -- rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres -- minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
